questions.question: What is history?
questions.correct_answer: History is the study of past events.
questions.explanation: Historians study past events to understand how societies changed over time.

questions.question: Why do we study history?
questions.correct_answer: To understand how people, societies and events changed over time.
questions.explanation: History helps us learn from past experiences and understand present society.

questions.question: What do historians use to study the past?
questions.correct_answer: Sources such as documents, books, letters and official records.
questions.explanation: These sources provide evidence about past events.

questions.question: Who are historians?
questions.correct_answer: People who study and write about the past.
questions.explanation: Historians collect evidence and interpret historical events.

questions.question: What are official records?
questions.correct_answer: Documents created by governments to record important events and activities.
questions.explanation: They help historians understand administrative and political history.

questions.question: What is meant by periodisation in history?
questions.correct_answer: Dividing history into different periods or time sections.
questions.explanation: This helps historians study historical developments more clearly.

questions.question: Who divided Indian history into Hindu, Muslim and British periods?
questions.explanation: He wrote the book 'A History of British India'.

questions.question: Why is James Mill’s periodisation criticised?
questions.correct_answer: Because it ignored the diversity and complexity of Indian society.
questions.explanation: Historians believe history cannot be divided only by religion.

questions.question: What are surveys in history?
questions.correct_answer: Investigations conducted to collect information about land, people and resources.
questions.explanation: The British conducted surveys to understand and control India better.

questions.question: Why did the British preserve official documents?
questions.correct_answer: To record administrative activities and maintain control over the country.
questions.explanation: These records helped the British govern India effectively.

notes.title: History
notes.content: History is the study of past events and helps us understand how societies, cultures and governments changed over time.

notes.title: Sources of History
notes.content: Historians study documents, letters, official records, maps and surveys to understand the past.

notes.title: Periodisation
notes.content: Historians divide history into periods to make the study of historical developments easier.

notes.title: Colonial Records
notes.content: The British government kept detailed records and documents to manage administration in India.

notes.title: Surveys
notes.content: The British conducted surveys such as revenue surveys and census surveys to collect information about land and people.

facts.fact: James Mill divided Indian history into Hindu, Muslim and British periods.
The British preserved large numbers of official records during their rule in India.
Surveys and maps were used by the British to understand and control India.
History helps us understand how societies changed over time.
